metadata.title: Comparative foreign-influence channels in the US (and allies), and the perception-vs-reality narrative layer (FARA spending; lobbying; campaign finance; institutional funding; influence/cyber ops)
metadata.frame: A deliberately neutral, evidence-graded comparison of how different FOREIGN STATES AND ORGANIZATIONS seek influence in the US (and allied states) - and the separate, documented question of how the PERCEPTION of each actor's influence is shaped. This is about states, lobbies, and operations and their disclosed CHANNELS - never about peoples or religions, and never asserting a 'control' conspiracy. The central, source-backed insight: different actors use different channels, so any single metric (FARA dollars, campaign finance, university gifts, or covert ops) ranks them differently - which is exactly why both 'X dominates everything' and 'X is just one of many' can mislead depending on the metric chosen. Legal lobbying, foreign-state media, institutional philanthropy, covert influence/cyber operations, and designated terror financing are EXPLICITLY DIFFERENT CATEGORIES and are not equated.
metadata.relevance: Connects the roster's foreign-money threads: Gulf AI capital [[fin-gulf-sovereign-ai-capital]] (Qatar QIA in xAI/Anthropic; Saudi PIF/UAE MGX), the FARA mechanism [[spec-cambodia-scam-fara-paid-influence]] / [[influence-congress-funding-compromise]], the contested pro-Israel-lobby claim already flagged in [[spec-uk-labour-tbi-influence]], the Epstein-Barak-Mandelson ties [[spec-epstein-document-authenticity]], and Iran's documented on-chain/cyber activity [[spec-onchain-threat-actor-addresses]] / [[geopolitics-contested-resource-states]].
metadata.sources: OpenSecrets Foreign Lobby Watch, Quincy Institute, Washington Examiner/Denver Gazette (FARA totals), Forward / Common Dreams / Military.com (AIPAC FARA debate + Massie bill), FDD / Quincy / Free Beacon / JPost (Qatar), DOJ indictment Sep 2024 + congressional CRS (Iran/IRGC), NCRI/IFMAT (contested NIAC claims). Full URLs in the rendered .md.

the_data_raw_spend.summary: By disclosed FARA spending, the top foreign spenders are NOT who popular perception assumes - and raw spend is a poor proxy for effective influence.

points.detail: Japan was the single largest FARA spender (~$48.5M, 2024); Saudi Arabia, China, and South Korea also outspent Israel and Qatar, which had once been the top foreign spenders. So by lobbying dollars, no single Middle East actor dominates.

China spent the most cumulatively (~$418M since 2016) but the vast majority funded two state-media outlets with negligible US audiences and almost nothing on traditional lobbying or campaigns - so the headline number overstates effective influence. The metric chosen drives the ranking.
DOJ FARA data omits unregistered foreign agents and does not capture domestic lobbies or campaign-finance channels - the largest gaps for actors that operate outside FARA (below).

channel_taxonomy.summary: Influence runs through at least six distinct channels; comparing actors requires naming the channel.

channels.who: Gulf states (Qatar, Saudi, UAE), Japan, South Korea
China (state outlets), Qatar (Al Jazeera - under a 2020 DOJ FARA order it has not complied with)

pro-Israel advocacy (AIPAC) - operates as a US domestic lobby and campaign-finance force, not a registered foreign agent; the focus of the recurring (and contested) FARA-registration debate and Rep. Massie's AIPAC Act
Qatar (~$4.7B to US universities 2001-2021; Brookings and other think tanks), China (Confucius Institutes, historically)
Iran (IRGC hack-and-leak vs the 2024 campaign, DOJ-indicted; assassination plots), Russia
ISIS, Hamas, and other US-designated groups - a LAW-ENFORCEMENT/sanctions category, categorically distinct from lobbying

per_actor.israel_pro_israel_lobby: Pro-Israel influence runs mainly through a DOMESTIC lobby (AIPAC) and campaign finance/endorsements, NOT FARA-registered foreign-agent lobbying - so it is large on the campaign-finance metric but not the top FARA spender. Whether AIPAC should register under FARA is a long-running, unresolved legal-political debate (Massie's bill; petitions that never gained DOJ traction); non-registration is a documented status, not proof of illegality. grade: fact (channel + debate); 'control' framing rejected.
per_actor.qatar: The clearest big-spender across MULTIPLE channels: ~$235-250M on FARA lobbying/PR since 2016 (the most FARA-registered meetings of any country, 2021-2025), ~$4.7B to US universities, think-tank funding (Brookings), and Al Jazeera (under an uncomplied-with 2020 DOJ FARA order). Ties the roster via QIA stakes in xAI and the Anthropic Series G. grade: fact.
per_actor.saudi_uae: Major FARA spenders AND the dominant Gulf AI-capital allocators (PIF/HUMAIN, MGX) - their influence is increasingly via capital deployment into the AI core ([[fin-gulf-sovereign-ai-capital]]) as much as lobbying. grade: fact.
per_actor.iran: Two very different things must be separated: (1) DOCUMENTED IRGC cyber/influence and coercion operations - e.g., the Sep-2024 DOJ indictment of IRGC members for a hack-and-leak against the Trump campaign, and assassination plots (fact); and (2) CONTESTED claims of an 'Iran lobby' (e.g., FARA allegations against NIAC) which are CRITIC-SOURCED and NOT DOJ findings (contested/unsupported). Conflating the two is a common error. grade: fact (ops) + contested (lobby claims).
per_actor.china: Heaviest cumulative FARA spend but concentrated in low-audience state media; little traditional lobbying/campaign activity; its real leverage is economic (chip/REE chokepoints, TikTok) not Washington lobbying. grade: fact.
per_actor.jihadist_groups: ISIS/Hamas-type influence is designated terror financing and propaganda - a sanctions/law-enforcement matter, NOT lobbying, and not comparable to the legal channels above. grade: fact (designations).

documented_specific_cases.summary: Concrete, named, on-the-record cases - one per channel - so the comparison rests on adjudicated/charged facts, not vibes.

cases.detail: DOJ indictment unsealed 4 Sep 2024 (SDNY): two RT employees (Kalashnikov, Afanasyeva) funneled ~$10M through Tennessee-based Tenet Media (owners Lauren Chen / Liam Donovan), which paid six US right-wing influencers (Tim Pool, Dave Rubin, Benny Johnson, Lauren Southern, Taylor Hanson, Matt Christiansen) for videos; an RT employee directed content. The influencers were not told the funder was RT. Charges: FARA + money-laundering conspiracy.

AIPAC's super PAC spent ~$9.9M to defeat Rep. Jamaal Bowman (the most expensive US House primary ever) and ~$8.6M against Rep. Cori Bush - the first two 'Squad' members to lose - as part of >$35.6M on congressional races and a >$100M cycle. This is LEGAL US campaign finance (not FARA, not illegal); it documents the scale of the domestic-lobby/campaign-finance channel.

An Israeli disinformation-for-hire industry (often ex-IDF/intel founders): Psy-Group's 'Project Butterfly' surveilled and targeted US BDS supporters for Israel's Ministry of Strategic Affairs (and drew 2016/Mueller interest); Team Jorge (Forbidden Stories 'Story Killers') boasted of meddling in 30+ elections with ~40,000 AI avatars; Archimedes Group and STOIC were taken down by Facebook/Meta for covert campaigns. Client attribution varies case-to-case (governments and private clients).
cases.grade: fact (Meta takedowns, Forbidden Stories, Mueller) + contested (per-case client attribution)

cases.detail: Qatar's gift of a ~$400M Boeing 747 to serve as Air Force One, slated to transfer to the Trump presidential-library foundation afterward - which ethics/constitutional-law scholars called a likely Foreign Emoluments Clause violation requiring congressional consent. A documented gift; legality contested.

cases.case: Flynn Intel Group / Bijan Kian; Ballard Partners
cases.detail: Bijan Kian (Flynn's partner) was convicted of covert lobbying for Turkey (2016, re Fethullah Gulen's extradition); Ballard Partners earned ~$4M (≈$2M Turkish embassy + ≈$2M from state-run Halkbank, later indicted in NY). A documented FARA-prosecution case.

China's spend concentrates in FARA-registered state media (low US audience) plus United Front / Confucius-Institute soft-power networks and periodic DOJ espionage/agent cases - leverage is economic (chips/REE/TikTok) more than Washington lobbying.

private_influence_industry.summary: A commercial 'influence-for-hire' sector now blurs the state/private line - the same capability sold to governments, parties, and corporations.

points.detail: Firms (notably Israeli - Team Jorge, Psy-Group, Archimedes, STOIC - but also Western PR firms doing Gulf FARA work) sell bot networks, fake-persona campaigns, hack-and-leak, and reputation attacks. Founded often by ex-intelligence officers; operate in a 'gray zone' advertising capability without spelling out illegality.

The same ecosystem includes commercial spyware (e.g., NSO Group's Pegasus, US-blacklisted 2021 after journalist/official targeting) - surveillance capability sold to states, adjacent to the influence-ops industry.
Commercialization means 'whose influence' is often a CONTRACTOR question, not a clean state-attribution - which both enables deniability and fuels misattribution in the perception layer below.

the_perception_layer.summary: Whose interest it serves for people to believe one actor is more (or less) influential than it is - the core perception question - is itself answerable from documented dynamics, without conspiracy.

points.detail: serves hawkish/defense and security-state interests (justifies budgets, sanctions, surveillance) and domestic political mobilization - real operations exist, but threat-inflation is a documented bureaucratic incentive.

cuts two ways: it can serve legitimate accountability/transparency arguments (the FARA-registration debate), but in bad-faith forms it shades into antisemitic 'control' conspiracy. The honest treatment keeps the legal-lobby-accountability question strictly separate from, and rejects, the conspiracy framing.

serves the influencer and its domestic partners - hence Qatar's preference for university/think-tank channels (low salience) and Al Jazeera's FARA non-compliance, and AIPAC's domestic-lobby posture (outside FARA disclosure).

Because actors use different channels, a single metric is weaponizable: 'Israel isn't even the top FARA spender' (true) and 'the Israel lobby dominates Congress' (true on campaign-finance/endorsement metrics) are both deployable to mislead. The corrective is to name the channel and the metric every time.

checks.result: Japan ~$48.5M #1; Saudi/China/South Korea outspent Israel & Qatar (OpenSecrets/Quincy/Washington Examiner).
Not FARA-registered; Massie AIPAC Act + recurring petitions; debate unresolved (Forward/Common Dreams/Military.com).
~$235-250M FARA since 2016; ~$4.7B to US universities; Al Jazeera 2020 DOJ FARA order uncomplied; QIA in xAI/Anthropic (FDD/Quincy/JPost).
DOJ Sep-2024 IRGC hack-and-leak indictment (fact); NIAC FARA allegations are critic-sourced, not DOJ findings (CRS/NCRI/IFMAT).

what_is_NOT_asserted.purpose: Explicit non-claims - essential for a charged topic.
non_claims: Nothing here concerns any PEOPLE or RELIGION - only states, governments, lobbies, and named organizations and their disclosed channels.
No 'control' or single-cabal conspiracy is asserted about ANY actor (Israel, the Gulf, Iran, China, or others).
AIPAC's non-registration under FARA is a documented legal-status/debate, NOT an assertion of illegality.
The 'Iran lobby'/NIAC FARA allegations are recorded as CONTESTED, critic-sourced claims - not DOJ findings - and no individual is accused.
Legal lobbying, state media, institutional philanthropy, covert ops, and designated terror financing are DIFFERENT CATEGORIES and are explicitly not equated or ranked on one scale.
These edges are OVERLAY/context (relationship / surveillance_security) and are EXCLUDED from the SCC / Z3 / TLA+ proofs.

limits: A neutral, evidence-graded comparison of foreign-influence CHANNELS (FARA lobbying, state media, domestic lobby + campaign finance, institutional funding, covert ops, designated terror financing) and the documented perception/narrative dynamics around them. Key facts: by FARA dollars Japan/Saudi/China/Korea/Qatar lead, not Israel (whose channel is a domestic lobby + campaign finance); raw spend overstates effective influence (China state media); Qatar spans the most channels; Iran's covert ops are DOJ-documented while 'Iran lobby' claims are contested. Different channels are not equated. Connects to the Gulf-AI-capital, FARA, Epstein-ties, and Iran-ops threads. Overlay edges excluded from the formal proofs. source: see verification_log and the rendered .md for full URLs.
